skill.name: plotly
skill.description: Interactive scientific and statistical data visualization library for Python. Use when creating charts, plots, or visualizations including scatter plots, line charts, bar charts, heatmaps, 3D plots, geographic maps, statistical distributions, financial charts, and dashboards. Supports both quick visualizations (Plotly Express) and fine-grained customization (graph objects). Outputs interactive HTML or static images (PNG, PDF, SVG).
skill.summary: Create interactive Plotly charts, dashboards, and exports from Python data.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium alerts, but review found they are documentation false positives from markdown fences and Plotly terminology. No prompt injection, hidden exfiltration, or executable malware behavior was found. The remaining risk is low and limited to documented installs, local exports, CDN use, and external links.

low_findings.title: Static external command detections are markdown examples
low_findings.description: The 293 Ruby backtick alerts are false positives caused by markdown code fences and inline Plotly examples. The files contain documentation examples, not executable skill code or command injection paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are markdown fences or documented Plotly usage snippets. I found no script file, dynamic command construction, or user-input command execution path.

low_findings.title: High-severity keyword alerts are benign Plotly terms
low_findings.description: The blocker alerts for C2 keywords, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance are keyword collisions in chart documentation. They refer to chart types, module names, option labels, or ordinary prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context shows ordinary visualization documentation, not cryptography, C2 behavior, or reconnaissance. No evidence found of malware intent or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Examples can install packages, write files, and use external resources
low_findings.description: The skill documents package installation, chart export to local files, CDN-backed HTML output, and external Plotly documentation links. These are expected for a Plotly guide but should be visible as low-risk operational behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write, install, CDN, and URL examples are directly present and legitimate for the skill domain. They are low risk because they are user-visible documentation, not hidden execution.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear charts for complex scientific and statistical data. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Plotly chart creation and export.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of scatter, line, bar, heatmap, map, 3D, statistical, and financial charts.
Explains when to use Plotly Express versus graph_objects for different visualization needs.
Provides patterns for layouts, subplots, templates, colors, axes, annotations, and shapes.
Documents interactive features such as hover text, zoom, range sliders, buttons, dropdowns, and animation.
Shows export paths for interactive HTML and static PNG, PDF, SVG, JPEG, and WebP outputs.
Includes Dash integration examples for embedding Plotly figures in simple web dashboards.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not include executable helper scripts.
Static image export requires Kaleido and a compatible Chrome or Chromium installation.
Some map styles may require external services or map provider tokens.
Generated chart quality still depends on the structure and cleanliness of the user data.

use_cases.title: Build Exploratory Charts
use_cases.description: Create quick Plotly Express charts from pandas DataFrames for inspection and communication.

use_cases.title: Prepare Scientific Figures
use_cases.description: Design publication-ready plots with custom layouts, annotations, subplots, and export formats.

use_cases.title: Prototype Dashboard Visuals
use_cases.description: Create interactive charts, controls, and Dash-ready figures for application prototypes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Chart
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner chart creation from a DataFrame
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plotly skill to create a clear chart for this DataFrame. Recommend the chart type and explain the main choices.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Chart Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose between Plotly Express and graph_objects
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plotly skill to compare Plotly Express and graph_objects for my visualization goal. Recommend the simpler reliable approach.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Interactive Figure
prompt_templates.scenario: Add hover, controls, ranges, and animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plotly skill to design an interactive figure with hover labels, useful controls, and export guidance for browser sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-View Dashboard Figure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced layout with multiple traces and exports
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Plotly skill to plan a multi-panel dashboard figure with subplots, consistent styling, annotations, and static and HTML export options.

output_examples.input: A CSV has monthly revenue, product category, and region columns.
output_examples.output: A recommended interactive line or grouped bar chart, with color by region and clear export instructions.

output_examples.input: A researcher needs a heatmap, 3D surface, and annotated threshold line.
output_examples.output: A figure plan using graph_objects, subplot guidance, color scale choices, and publication export notes.

output_examples.input: A dashboard prototype needs filters, range sliders, and hover details.
output_examples.output: A Plotly interaction plan with range controls, custom hover text, responsive HTML export, and Dash embedding notes.

best_practices: Start with Plotly Express for standard charts, then customize the returned figure when needed.
Choose export settings early so image size, resolution, and HTML dependencies match the use case.
Keep hover text, legends, labels, and color scales consistent across related charts.
anti_patterns: Do not use graph_objects for simple charts when Plotly Express provides the same result faster.
Do not export CDN-based HTML when the chart must work without internet access.
Do not rely on default labels when the chart will be shared outside the analysis team.

faq.question: Does this skill run Plotly code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for creating Plotly visualizations in Python.

faq.question: Can it create both interactive and static charts?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers interactive HTML output and static image export formats such as PNG, PDF, and SVG.

faq.question: When should I use Plotly Express?
faq.answer: Use Plotly Express for common chart types, tidy DataFrames, and fast visual exploration.

faq.question: When should I use graph_objects?
faq.answer: Use graph_objects for complex traces, mixed subplots, custom shapes, detailed layout control, or specialized charts.

faq.question: Does static export need extra dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes. Static export usually needs Kaleido, and recent Kaleido versions require Chrome or Chromium.

faq.question: Can it help with dashboards?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for multi-plot figures, interactivity, responsive HTML, and basic Dash integration.
